title: Arctium × rehmannii
taxonomy: Arctium × rehmannii is classified as a hybrid species within the genus Arctium, which is a group of thistle plants in the family Asteraceae. It is often referred to as a cultivated variety of the common burdock plant, known for its edible roots and leaves. As a hybrid, its classification is often debated, with some sources considering it a subspecies of Arctium lappa. However, others recognize it as a distinct species due to its unique characteristics and morphology. The classification of Arctium × rehmannii can vary depending on the taxonomic system used.

intro: Arctium × rehmannii, a sterile hybrid of the greater burdock (Arctium lappa) and the lance-leaf burdock (Arctium lanceolatum), is a biennial plant native to Europe and Asia, known for its edible leaves and roots. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation and improving digestive health. The bioactive constituents of Arctium × rehmannii include inulin, arctiin, and arctigenin, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, as well as in traditional European and Asian medicine to support overall health and well-being.
